from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a textual fragment. The placeholder {subj} and {obj} will be filled.
    verb_map = {
        "league": "{subj} plays in the {obj} league",
        "manager": "{subj} is managed by {obj}",
        "position": "{subj} holds the position of {obj}",
        "ground": "{subj} plays at {obj}",
        "birthDate": "{subj} was born on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "{subj} was born in {obj}",
        "birthYear": "{subj} was born in {obj}",
        "club": "{subj} plays for {obj}",
        "draftTeam": "{subj} was drafted by {obj}",
        "height": "{subj} has a height of {obj} meters",
        "youthclub": "{subj} started at youth club {obj}",
        "currentclub": "{subj} currently plays for {obj}",
        "weight": "{subj} weighs {obj} kilograms",
        "deathPlace": "{subj} died in {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "{subj} started his active years in {obj}",
        "currentteam": "{subj} currently is on the team {obj}",
        "debutTeam": "{subj} debuted with {obj}",
        "draftPick": "{subj} was picked #{obj}",
        "draftRound": "{subj} was drafted in round {obj}",
        "formerTeam": "{subj} formerly played for {obj}",
        "playerNumber": "{subj} wears number {obj}",
        "college": "{subj} attended {obj}",
        "draftYear": "{subj} was drafted in {obj}",
        "coach": "{subj} is coached by {obj}",
        "owner": "{subj} is owned by {obj}",
        "city": "{subj} is based in {obj}",
        "leader": "{subj} is led by {obj}",
        "anthem": "{subj} has the anthem \"{obj}\"",
        "ethnicGroup": "{subj} has an ethnic group of {obj}",
        "language": "{subj} speaks {obj}",
        "generalManager": "{subj} is managed by {obj}",
        "chairman": "{subj} is chaired by {obj}",
        "season": "{subj} participated in the season {obj}",
        "currency": "{subj} uses the currency {obj}",
        "demonym": "people from {subj} are called {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "{subj}'s official language is {obj}",
        "stadium": "{subj}'s home stadium is {obj}",
        "universityTeam": "{subj}'s university team is {obj}",
        "mostChampions": "{subj} has the most championships: {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subj}'s leader title is {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subj} is part of {obj}",
        "country": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "utcOffset": "{subj} has a UTC offset of {obj}",
        "timeZone": "{subj} is in the {obj} time zone",
        "areaTotal": "{subj} covers an area of {obj} square kilometres",
        "foundingDate": "{subj} was founded on {obj}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause
    subj_map = {}
    for triple in triples:
        subj_map.setdefault(triple.subject, []).append(triple)

    subject_sentences = []
    for subj, triple_list in subj_map.items():
        parts = []
        for triple in triple_list:
            pattern = verb_map.get(triple.predicate)
            if pattern:
                part = pattern.format(subj=triple.subject, obj=triple.object)
            else:
                # Fallback generic rendering
                part = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}"
            parts.append(part)
        # Combine parts that share the same subject
        if len(parts) == 1:
            subject_sentences.append(parts[0])
        else:
            subject_sentences.append(", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1])

    if not subject_sentences:
        return ""

    # Combine different subjects into one fluent sentence
    if len(subject_sentences) == 1:
        sentence = subject_sentences[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(subject_sentences[:-1]) + "; and " + subject_sentences[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
